title: Axon Draft One Records (Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records related to the use of any AI-assisted police report technology provided by the company Axon (previously known as Taser). This technology may be referred to as Draft One (https://www.axon.com/products/draft-one), the Axon Narrative Assistant, or by another name. 

For more information on the Axon Narrative Assistant, please refer to the User Guide found at this link: https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/25418151-axon-narrative-assistant-user-guide_axon-records-1-1/. 

For more information on Axon Draft One, please look at this page on the Axon website: https://www.axon.com/resources/closer-look-draft-one. 

CONTRACT AND AGREEMENTS

1.  All contracts, memorandums of understanding, and any other written agreements between this agency and Axon related to the use of Draft One, Narrative Assistant, or any other AI-assisted report generation tool provided by Axon. 

Responsive records include all associated amendments, exhibits, and supplemental and supporting documentation. 

If access to Draft One or similar tools is being provided via an existing contract or through an informal agreement, please provide the relevant contract or the relevant communication or agreement that facilitated the access. This includes all agreements, both formal and informal, including all trial access, even if that access does not or did not involve financial obligations. 

2. All relevant terms of use, licensing agreements, and any other guiding materials;

TRAINING, POLICIES and USE MATERIAL

3. All training materials relevant to Draft One or Axon Narrative Assistant generated by this agency; 

4. All training material provided by Axon to this agency regarding its use of Draft One; 

5. All user manuals, other guidance materials, help documents, or related materials;

6. Guides, safety tests, and other supplementary material that mention Draft One provided by Axon from January 1, 2024 and the date this request is received;

7. All final reports, evaluations, reports, or other documentation concluding or summarizing a trial or evaluation period or pilot project; 

8. Any and all policies related to the use of Draft One, the Narrative Assistant, or any other AI-assisted report generation offerings provided by Axon (An example of one such policy can be found here: https://cdn.muckrock.com/foia_files/2024/11/26/608_Computer_Software_and_Transcription-Assisted_Report_Generation.pdf);


COMMUNICATIONS
9. All communications sent or received by any representative of this agency with individuals representing Axon referencing the following term, including emails and attachments: 
• Draft One
• Narrative Assistant
• AI-generated report

10. All communications sent to or received by any representative of this agency with each of the following email addresses, including attachments:
a. draftone@axon.com
b. bryan@axon.com
c. noahsw@axon.com               	 
d. efleischli@axon.com (Erin Fleischli)
e. edriver@axon.com
f. eforester@axon.com (Evan Forester, Director of Product Marketing)    
g. gsigman@axon.com (Gabriel Sigman, Technical Account Manager)   	

We ask that these materials be provided on a rolling basis; as your office collects and reviews records, we would appreciate it if your office would provide those materials to us incrementally. If possible, please provide any responsive contractual and financial materials within the statutorily-mandated public record law time frame. We would prefer communications to be provided next. Please contact us for further discussion regarding the search and release of these materials.

This request is not being made for commercial purposes. Please assess fees appropriately in accordance with a public records request being made in the public interest.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform us of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I request that all records be disclosed in their native, digital format and transmitted to us electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or by CD-ROM if not.

If you need any more information in order to process the request, or cannot process a portion of the request for any reason, please respond to the email address on this request or “aos@eff.org”. Further discussion may be conducted via email. Please let us know if a phone call will be necessary or desirable; such contact information will be provided upon request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires business days.

Sincerely, 
Jesse Cabrera

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Non Profit Sales Tax Exemptions
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to Florida's Public Records Act (Chapter 119, Florida Statutes, 
also known as the Sunshine Law), I respectfully request the following 
public records from the Florida Department of Revenue:

A list of all nonprofit organizations currently holding a valid 
Consumer's Certificate of Exemption (Form DR-14) issued by the 
Department under Florida Statutes §212.08(7) and Rule 12A-1.038 F.A.C., 
as of the most recent date for which complete data is available.

For each organization, please include the following data fields where 
maintained by the Department:

  1. Organization legal name
  2. Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN / FEIN)
  3. Consumer's Certificate of Exemption number
  4. Mailing address (street, city, state, ZIP — minimum: city, state, ZIP)
  5. Exemption category (e.g., charitable, religious, educational, 
     scientific, parent-teacher organization, veterans' organization, 
     volunteer fire department, library, etc., per the categories listed 
     in §212.08(7))
  6. Date of original DR-14 issuance
  7. Current certificate effective date
  8. Current certificate expiration date (DR-14 certificates expire 
     after five years per the Department's renewal process)
  9. Current status (active, expired, revoked, suspended)
  10. NTEE or other organization-type classification code, if maintained

Please provide this information in CSV, Excel, or another machine-readable 
electronic format. If the source data is maintained in a database, an 
export from that database in its native format is strongly preferred 
over a re-keyed list or PDF report.

Basis for non-confidentiality at the individual-organization level:
The Department maintains multiple public-facing verification channels 
that allow any member of the public to confirm a specific organization's 
DR-14 exemption status:

  - The online Certificate Verification System at 
    floridarevenue.com/taxes/certificates
  - The FL Tax-Verify mobile application
  - The Department's automated nationwide toll-free telephone verification 
    system at 1(877)357-3725

These three publicly available verification mechanisms demonstrate that 
DR-14 status determinations are treated as non-confidential at the 
individual-organization level and are routinely disclosed by the 
Department on request. The records I am requesting are the same status 
determinations, provided in bulk format for efficiency.

To the extent the Department interprets F.S. §213.053 (taxpayer 
information confidentiality) to cover any of the requested fields, 
please note that §213.053 protects "information contained in returns, 
reports, accounts, or declarations received by the department," and 
that administrative status determinations issued by the Department 
under §212.08(7) are distinct from return information. The Department's 
practice of publicly verifying individual certificates further 
demonstrates that DR-14 status information falls outside §213.053's 
confidentiality scope. If the Department interprets §213.053 to cover 
any specific requested field, please identify the field(s) and provide 
the remaining fields separately rather than withholding the entire 
record. Per F.S. §119.07(1)(d), non-exempt portions of responsive 
records must be released even when some portions may be exempt.

I am requesting this information for research purposes 
relating to nonprofit-sector regulatory analysis. If any fee in excess 
of $50 is anticipated, please notify me before proceeding so that scope 
or format can be adjusted to reduce cost. Per F.S. §119.07(4)(d), I 
understand the Department may charge for the actual cost of duplication 
plus, where extensive use of agency resources is required, a reasonable 
service charge.

Per F.S. §119.07(1)(c), please acknowledge receipt of this request 
promptly and identify a reasonable time within which responsive records 
will be provided. If any portion of this request is denied, please 
provide the specific statutory basis for each denial under §119.07(1)(f).

Thank you for your assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

non-profit-researcher

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Non Profit Sales Tax Exemptions
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to New York's Freedom of Information Law (Public Officers Law 
Article 6, §§84-90), I respectfully request the following public records 
from the New York State Department of Taxation and Finance:

A list of all organizations currently holding a valid Form ST-119 Exempt 
Organization Certificate issued by the Department under Tax Law §1116 
and 20 NYCRR Part 529, as of the most recent date for which complete 
data is available.

For each organization, please include the following data fields where 
maintained by the Department:

  1. Organization legal name
  2. Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN / FEIN)
  3. Six-digit New York State sales tax exemption number
  4. Mailing address (street, city, state, ZIP — minimum: city, state, ZIP)
  5. Exempt organization category (charitable, educational, religious,
     scientific, etc.)
  6. Date of original ST-119 issuance
  7. Current status (active, expired, revoked, suspended)
  8. NTEE or other organization-type classification code, if maintained

Please provide this information in CSV, Excel, or another machine-readable 
electronic format. If the source data is maintained in a database, an 
export from that database in its native format is preferred over a 
re-keyed list or PDF report.

Basis for non-confidentiality at the individual level:
The Department's published guidance at tax.ny.gov/bus/st/exempt.htm 
instructs members of the public to contact the sales tax information 
center to verify any organization's exempt status. This practice 
demonstrates that ST-119 status determinations are treated as 
non-confidential at the individual-organization level and are 
routinely disclosed by the Department on request. The records I am 
requesting are the same status determinations, requested in bulk for 
efficiency.

Tax Law §697 confidentiality protections apply to tax returns and 
return information. Status determinations under §1116 are 
administrative determinations distinct from return information and are 
not within the §697 confidentiality scope. (See 20 NYCRR §2392.1 
distinguishing administrative records from return information.) If the 
Department interprets §697 to cover any of the requested fields, please 
identify the specific field(s) and provide the remaining fields 
separately rather than withholding the entire record. Per POL §89(4), 
a record is not exempt from disclosure simply because some portion of 
it might be exempt — non-exempt portions must be disclosed.

I am requesting this information for research purposes 
relating to nonprofit-sector regulatory analysis. If any fee in excess 
of $150 is anticipated, please notify me before proceeding so that scope 
or format can be adjusted to reduce cost.

Per POL §89(3), please acknowledge receipt of this request within five 
business days and indicate when responsive records will be made 
available. If the request is denied in whole or in part, please provide 
the specific statutory basis for each denial.

Thank you for your assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

non-profit-researcher

title: Jefferson County (CO) Sheriffs Office (CISC Member) - Accurint/LexisNexis Data Sharing

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

In regards to your agency's LexisNexis/Accurint Data sharing scope - We are seeking to understand:
1) Does your agency contribute data to LexisNexis/Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC)/PSDEX?
2) If so, do you limit which LexisNexis AVCC customers your data is shared with?
3) If you do limit data sharing by agency, what agencies have access to JCSO data via AVCC?

communications.subject: FW: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: RFP List
communications.communication:
Good afternoon:

This is in response to your email correspondence below. Please be advised that the City construes your request for records as a request coming under New Hampshire’s Right-to-Know law, RSA chapter 91-A.
Please be further advised that the City has no obligation to respond to requests from out-of-state corporations unless the corporation can show that it is located and/or has a nexus in New Hampshire. See McBurney v. Young, 569 U.S. 221 (2013). The City receives a large number of requests from out-of-state data collection corporations requesting financial and other reports to be submitted electronically to an email address and/or website. Due to the significant amount of time it takes for staff to research and generate reports in response to the out-of-state requests from data collection companies, the City will not transmit responses to such requests.

Please note that the City is required to make documents which are subject to disclosure, accessible for public review on its regular business premises (RSA Chapter 91-A:4,I). In accordance with RSA 91-A, the City would provide you, or a Representative (Mr. Van Fleet), the opportunity to personally review records, free of charge, at City Hall.  To the extent that your Representative would request copies of any documents in electronic format, your Representative may bring a sealed/unopened thumb drive and the City will upload the documents to the thumb drive, free of charge. If your Representative does not have a sealed/unopened thumb drive at the time of the scheduled visit to City Hall, we have new thumb drives that may be purchased at cost price.

Please contact our office at 603-225-8505 to schedule a date and time for you to review the documents at City Hall. The City will require approximately thirty (30) days prior notice of the date of when you intend to review the documents so that we can have the documents ready for your review upon your arrival.

Notwithstanding the aforementioned, please be advised that you may find information you are seeking on the City’s website at https://www.concordnh.gov/1092/Bids-Proposals-Quotations.

Thank you,

Velinda Diaz
Paralegal

title: Washington County Public Schools Open Records Request - Student enrollment data

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Washington County Public Schools Open Records Request - Student enrollment data
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records Coordinator,

I am writing on behalf of the Data Acquisition Lab to formally request access to public records under the Maryland Public Information Act, Title 4. We are requesting aggregated counts of student enrollment at the most granular geographic level available, which will enable a more meaningful understanding of community-level trends. We plan to request this information on a bi-annual basis going forward.

I am specifically requesting a tabular dataset containing the counts of enrolled students in the Washington County Public Schools for the current (2025-2026) school year, disaggregated by the following fields:
- School name
- United States Census Tract
- Grade level (Pre-K through 12, or the finest granularity available for each school)

Please provide this data in electronic format (CSV preferred), including the following fields where available:
- School identifier (name, NCES ID)
- Census Tract GEOID (full 11-digit or abbreviated 6-digit code)
- Grade level
- Student enrollment count

Privacy Considerations:
- This request does not seek, nor does it require, personally identifiable information (PII) or any data points that could reasonably be used to identify individual students or households.
- We respect the importance of compliance with all applicable privacy laws. If additional thresholds or adjustments are required to ensure absolute privacy, we are happy to collaborate on refinements to the data as necessary.

If certain records or portions of records are exempt from disclosure, please provide a written explanation of the specific exemption(s) and release any non-exempt portions of the requested records. If this data is not centrally held, please advise which agency holds this data. Thank you for your attention to this request. If you have any questions or require further information, please feel free to contact me at request@applieddataproject.org.
